meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve no marketing skills (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no marketing skills. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Portfolio Building to Solve No Marketing Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to leverage portfolio building to overcome marketing barriers. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for developers.
content.answer_block: You don't need a marketing budget; you need a high-signal portfolio. By building specific, problem-solving AI tools and documenting the technical architecture, you attract recruiters and users organically. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate on production-ready features that prove your technical authority to the world.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hundreds of hours mastering React, Python, and LLM integrations, yet the void of the internet remains indifferent. The 'no marketing skills' trap is a rite of passage for every developer, but it is also a false narrative. You don't need to be a growth hacker; you need to be a builder who makes their work impossible to ignore.

This case study flips the script. Instead of chasing vanity metrics, we’ll use strategic portfolio building to create a gravitational pull toward your work. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you will transform your GitHub from a graveyard of tutorials into a high-converting engine that demonstrates your engineering maturity to potential employers and collaborators.

steps.title: Define Your Niche Problem
steps.description: Identify a specific workflow bottleneck in a vertical like legal, health, or logistics. This provides the 'Why' behind your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of 5 technical pain points for small business owners that can be solved with an AI-agentic workflow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research and document 3 high-impact project ideas based on current AI API capabilities.'

steps.title: Architect the MVP with Antigravity
steps.description: Scaffold a robust, scalable architecture that prioritizes clean code and modularity over quick hacks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a boilerplate project structure for a FastAPI backend and a Next.js frontend, including containerization and CI/CD config.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity boilerplate.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to rapidly write the business logic, ensuring your code is clean, documented, and production-grade.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular service layer for handling LLM streaming responses and database interactions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Implement the core service layer and ensure type safety for all API endpoints.'

steps.title: Build 'Show, Don't Tell' Documentation
steps.description: Automate the creation of a 'Case Study' README that focuses on the engineering challenges and your solutions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional project README that highlights the system architecture, performance metrics, and the problem solved.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a detailed technical README.md documenting the design patterns used in this project.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your project to a live environment so users can interact with it, proving your ability to deliver end-to-end solutions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a script to automate deployment to Vercel and Supabase integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure environment variables and setup production deployment script.'

pro_tips: Focus your README on the 'Why' behind your architectural decisions, not just the code snippets.
Include a 'Performance Metrics' section in your repository to show you understand scale and latency.
Use Claude Code to perform a self-code review to identify potential security vulnerabilities before pushing.
Build a 'system diagram' using Mermaid.js in your documentation to prove your architectural thinking.
Update your LinkedIn profile with a link to your project, but frame it as a 'Technical Case Study' rather than a 'Side Project'.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your project on specialized developer forums like Hacker News or Reddit's r/sideproject. Reach out to three people in your target industry for feedback, and use their critique to iterate on version 2.0.
content.conversion_block: You are only one project away from establishing your technical authority. Stop waiting for permission to be noticed; start building the evidence that proves your expertise. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
